verses.1: Better is a poor person who walks in his integrity Than a person who is perverse in speech and is a fool.
verses.2: Also it is not good for a person to be without knowledge, And one who hurries his footsteps errs.
verses.3: The foolishness of a person ruins his way, And his heart rages against the LORD.
verses.4: Wealth adds many friends, But a poor person is separated from his friend.
verses.5: A false witness will not go unpunished, And one who declares lies will not escape.
verses.6: Many will seek the favor of a generous person, And every person is a friend to him who gives gifts.
verses.7: All the brothers of a poor person hate him; How much more do his friends abandon him! He pursues them with words, but they are gone.
verses.8: One who gets wisdom loves his own soul; One who keeps understanding will find good.
verses.9: A false witness will not go unpunished, And one who declares lies will perish.
verses.10: Luxury is not fitting for a fool; Much less for a slave to rule over princes.
verses.11: A person’s discretion makes him slow to anger, And it is his glory to overlook an offense.
verses.12: A king’s wrath is like the roaring of a lion, But his favor is like dew on the grass.
verses.13: A foolish son is destruction to his father, And the quarrels of a wife are a constant dripping.
verses.14: House and wealth are an inheritance from fathers, But a prudent wife is from the LORD.
verses.15: Laziness casts one into a deep sleep, And a lazy person will suffer hunger.
verses.16: One who keeps the commandment keeps his soul, But one who is careless of conduct will die.
verses.17: One who is gracious to a poor person lends to the LORD, And He will repay him for his good deed.
verses.18: Discipline your son while there is hope, And do not desire his death.
verses.19: A person of great anger will suffer the penalty, For if you rescue him, you will only have to do it again.
verses.20: Listen to advice and accept discipline, So that you may be wise the rest of your days.
verses.21: Many plans are in a person’s heart, But the advice of the LORD will stand.
verses.22: What is desirable in a person is his kindness, And it is better to be a poor person than a liar.
verses.23: The fear of the LORD leads to life, So that one may sleep satisfied, untouched by evil.
verses.24: The lazy one buries his hand in the dish, But will not even bring it back to his mouth.
verses.25: Strike a scoffer and the naive may become clever, But rebuke one who has understanding, and he will gain knowledge.
verses.26: He who assaults his father and drives his mother away Is a shameful and disgraceful son.
verses.27: Stop listening, my son, to discipline, And you will stray from the words of knowledge.
verses.28: A worthless witness makes a mockery of justice, And the mouth of the wicked swallows wrongdoing.
verses.29: Judgments are prepared for scoffers, And beatings for the backs of fools.
